title: Scrapy Architecture, Scheduler, Middleware, AutoThrottle, and Observability · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed crawl review reconstructs scrapy architecture, scheduler, middleware, autothrottle, and observability from authorization, revision, seed, URL key, response identity, robots decision, parse record, extraction provenance, frontier event, report row, failure, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for Scrapy 2.17 scale and observability transfer plan after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, ca…
objectives: Trace engine, scheduler, downloader, spider, item pipeline, signals, extensions, requests, responses, failures, and ownership in Scrapy.
Map canonical request identity, priorities, persistence, depth, retries, duplicate filtering, and resume semantics into scheduler evidence.
Reason about request and response traversal order, built-in middleware, exceptions, short-circuiting, and settings merge before customization.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

stimulus.title: Crawl Observability case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review site-quality team handles case 6459 during a clean package and recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to map the crawler into Scrapy architecture, scheduler, middleware, AutoThrottle, and reconstructable telemetry; no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted; then introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it. lines.label: risk
lines.text: Aggregate request and item counts are sufficient production diagnostics.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Correlate crawl, request, response, page, extraction, queue, retry, block, privacy, output, and recovery events with bounded labels and redaction. Artifact under review: Scrapy 2.17 scale and observability transfer plan.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Crawl Observability, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.
